obligations.reason: Visible RTL contains a complete JTAG/DMI debug path, jtag_key/pass_chk signals, and debug control outputs. The analysis should verify that debug commands cannot reach hart-control outputs unless the authentication/authorization guard is actually used on the command path.
obligations.subject: external debug requester
obligations.operation: trigger halt/resume or enter debug mode
obligations.object: hart debug entry/control state (debug_req_o, haltreq_o, resumereq_o, debug_mode_q, dpc/dscratch debug CSRs)
obligations.expected_guard: DMI/JTAG operations that can halt/resume harts or enter debug mode should be gated by an explicit debug authentication/authorization condition, such as pass_chk/jtag_key validation and/or lifecycle/test authorization, before asserting debug_req_o/haltreq_o/resumereq_o.
obligations.candidate_path: JTAG TAP -> dmi_jtag -> dm_top/dm_csrs -> debug control outputs -> csr_regfile debug_req_i/debug_mode_q
state_conditions_to_check: cmdbusy or error handling during command issuance
required_evidence_slots: subject: external JTAG/DMI requester via tck/tms/td_i or SimJTAG/SimDTM
operation: DMI write/read selecting debug control registers or issuing commands
object: haltreq/resumereq/debug_req/debug_mode state
expected guard: pass_chk/jtag_key/lifecycle/test authorization
observed behavior: RTL condition driving request/control outputs
path/reachability: TAP to DMI to dm_csrs/dm_top to csr_regfile
source locations
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer lists jtag_key and pass_chk but does not show assignments or whether they gate all DMI operations.

obligations.reason: dm_csrs exposes sbaddress_o, sbdata_o, sbreadonaddr_o, sbreadondata_o, sbdata_write_valid_o and dm_top instantiates dm_sba with AXI connectivity. Debug SBA is a high-risk alternate master path requiring authorization and address mediation.
obligations.subject: debug module system bus master
obligations.operation: read/write system bus via debug SBA
obligations.object: system bus memory and MMIO reachable through sbaddress/sbdata and AXI master signals
obligations.expected_guard: System bus access from the debug module should be authorized by debug authentication and constrained by address/range/access-policy checks or SoC fabric permissions before reads/writes to protected memory/MMIO regions are issued.
obligations.candidate_path: DMI/JTAG -> dm_csrs system bus access registers -> dm_sba in dm_top -> AXI master interface -> SoC memory/peripherals
state_conditions_to_check: sbbusy_i handling and writes during active transfers
AXI address width/truncation or bridge decode behavior
required_evidence_slots: subject: DMI/JTAG requester
operation: SBA read or write through sbaddress/sbdata registers
object: AXI-addressed memory/peripheral region
expected guard: debug auth plus range/policy check
observed behavior: conditions creating req/we/addr/wdata on dm_sba/AXI
path/reachability: DMI register writes to AXI master interface
impact: ability to read/write protected regions if unmediated

obligations.uncertainty: The dm_sba module implementation is instantiated but not present in the fact-layer file list; analysis may need to inspect available connections and any imported implementation.

obligations.reason: dm_csrs exposes command-valid, data paths, hart status, and selected_hart state. The analysis should check that command execution and data exposure are not driven merely by DMI writes without authorization and target-state mediation.
obligations.subject: external DMI requester
obligations.operation: execute abstract command or read/write debug data
obligations.object: hart architectural/debug state reachable through cmd_valid_o, data_o/data_i, cmderror, halted/resumeack status
obligations.expected_guard: Abstract debug commands and data register accesses should require active authenticated debug mode, target-hart availability/selection checks, and command-busy/error mediation before exposing or modifying hart/register state.
obligations.candidate_path: DMI/JTAG -> dm_csrs command/data registers -> abstract command outputs -> hart/debug memory interface
required_evidence_slots: subject: DMI/JTAG requester
operation: command/data register read or write
object: selected hart register/debug state
expected guard: authenticated active debug session plus halted/available/cmdready conditions
observed behavior: cmd_valid_o/data_o/data_valid_i/cmd error logic
path/reachability: DMI case decode to command/data outputs
source locations
obligations.uncertainty: The fact layer shows command/data/control ports but not the decode details or downstream abstract command implementation.

obligations.reason: csr_regfile has CSR address/data inputs, read_access_exception, csr_we, privilege-related umode_i, debug_mode_q, and many protected CSR state registers/outputs that affect trap routing, translation, interrupts, debug, and cache behavior. The visible RTL should be checked for complete privilege mediation at decode and write sites.
obligations.subject: software executing CSR operations
obligations.object: privileged CSRs: mtvec, medeleg, mideleg, mie/mip, mstatus-derived controls, satp/asid, dpc, dscratch0/1, cache-enable CSRs, performance CSRs
obligations.expected_guard: Writes and reads of privileged CSRs should be mediated by current privilege/mode checks (including umode_i, debug_mode_q, CSR address privilege encoding, read/write access exceptions) and by debug-mode-only rules for dpc/dscratch/dcsr-like state.
obligations.candidate_path: csr_regfile CSR decode/write path (csr_addr_i, csr_we, csr_wdata_i) -> machine/supervisor/debug CSRs and permission outputs
state_conditions_to_check: umode_i and any supervisor/machine privilege indicators
read_access_exception and illegal write exception generation
csr_we gating for all CSR update cases
required_evidence_slots: subject: software CSR instruction path with current privilege/debug state
operation: CSR read/write
object: specific privileged CSR/register/output
expected guard: privilege level/debug mode/CSR access exception
observed behavior: decode/write-enable/read-mux conditions
path/reachability: csr_addr_i to state update/read data
impact: changed access policy, trap vector, translation or debug state
source locations
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer only lists selected CSR-related signals; exact privilege decode must be inspected in source.

obligations.reason: The CSR regfile exports multiple signals that directly control address translation and supervisor/user memory permissions. The analysis should verify that their update paths are restricted by privilege and that mode transitions cannot unintentionally enable unsafe access policy.
obligations.subject: software CSR requester
obligations.object: virtual-memory and memory-permission policy state (satp_ppn_o, asid_o, en_translation_o, en_ld_st_translation_o, SUM, MXR, MPRV, TVM/TW/TSR)
obligations.expected_guard: Only appropriately privileged software should modify translation and memory-access policy controls; supervisor actions that machine mode can restrict via TVM/TW/TSR-like controls should be blocked when the corresponding restriction bits are set.
state_conditions_to_check: mret/sret updates to privilege and MPRV-like behavior
required_evidence_slots: subject: software CSR write or return-from-trap operation
operation: write/modify translation policy CSR or mode bits
object: translation/access policy outputs
expected guard: machine/supervisor privilege and TVM/TW/TSR restrictions
observed behavior: update/use of satp, mstatus bits, en_translation logic
path/reachability: CSR decode to output signals
source locations
obligations.uncertainty: Privilege-mode representation beyond umode_i is not fully visible in the fact layer.

obligations.reason: The fact layer shows multiple reset domains and debug reset controls: dmi_rst_ni, dmi_reset_o, dmactive_o, ndmreset_o, test_logic_reset, rst_ni, and internal SBA/data registers. Reset/lifecycle handling is a common source of permission bypass or stale exposure.
obligations.subject: debug/JTAG reset controller
obligations.operation: reset, activate/deactivate debug module, clear debug state
obligations.object: debug module active/reset state, DMI request/response state, SBA address/data registers, hart debug state
obligations.expected_guard: External debug reset controls should be authorized and should not leave security-sensitive debug/SBA state active or stale after dmactive deassertion, dmi reset, non-debug reset, or ndmreset transitions.
obligations.candidate_path: DMI/JTAG reset controls -> dm_csrs ndmreset_o/dmactive_o/dmi_reset -> top-level resets and csr_regfile/debug state
state_conditions_to_check: dmactive_o reset default and clear behavior
required_evidence_slots: subject: external JTAG/DMI requester or reset source
operation: assert/reset/clear debug module state
object: debug/SBA/control/data state
expected guard: authorization plus secure reset defaults/clearing
observed behavior: reset assignments and state retention
path/reachability: JTAG reset/DMI reset to affected registers and outputs
source locations
obligations.uncertainty: Reset assignments are not included in the fact layer; source inspection required.

obligations.reason: The fact layer shows a jtag_key output from ariane_peripherals, a jtag_key input to dmi_jtag, ROM2 signals including pkey_loc_o, and APB-to-reg instances. The analysis should check whether the key source or key-location is writable/readable by unprivileged MMIO paths or has unsafe reset constants.
obligations.subject: software/MMIO requester and external JTAG requester
obligations.operation: configure/read/expose debug authentication key material or key pointer
obligations.object: jtag_key and any ROM2/public-key/key-location data controlling debug authentication
obligations.expected_guard: The JTAG authentication key or key-location/configuration registers should be protected from unauthorized APB/MMIO writes and reads, with reset defaults that do not enable debug access by default.
obligations.candidate_path: ariane_peripherals APB/reg interfaces -> jtag_key output and ROM2 pkey_loc_o/rom2_rdata -> dmi_jtag jtag_key/pass_chk
state_conditions_to_check: whether jtag_key can be overwritten after boot or after lock
whether key value is readable on APB or other outputs
ROM2 we_i handling for supposed read-only key/code content

expected guard: privileged/locked write access and non-exposure of secret key
observed behavior: APB decode/register update/read mux and pass_chk derivation

obligations.reason: rom2 has req_i, we_i, addr_i, wdata_i, and rdata_o despite being named ROM, and ariane_peripherals connects rom2_we/rom2_wdata/rom2_addr. The analysis should check whether write attempts are blocked and whether address decode restricts access to intended readers/writers.
obligations.subject: SoC bus/APB requester

obligations.expected_guard: ROM2 or boot/key material should be read-only or write-protected for unauthorized masters; any write enable reaching rom2.we_i should be ignored or mediated by a privileged lifecycle/debug/programming guard.
obligations.candidate_path: APB/AXI fabric in ariane_peripherals/testharness -> ROM2 instance -> rdata_o/pkey_loc_o and we_i

whether writes can affect pkey_loc_o/jtag_key-related data

obligations.reason: ariane_peripherals contains APB-to-reg bridges for AES and packet-related interfaces with aes_prdata/aes_pwdata/aes_pwrite/aes_psel and pkt_* signals. Even if implementation modules are external, visible wrapper decode should be checked for whether protected registers are globally accessible or mediated.

obligations.expected_guard: Security-sensitive peripheral register banks, especially AES/key/config/status paths, should require intended privilege/address mediation and should avoid exposing secret key/intermediate/plaintext state through APB read data unless explicitly permitted.

obligations.reason: The fact layer shows multiple AXI connect instances, debug master/slave wiring, AXI clock converters, SPI/Ethernet AXI slave signals including awprot/arprot-like attributes. The analysis should check address-range enforcement and security attribute preservation at visible wrapper boundaries.

obligations.expected_guard: Bus fabric should preserve requester identity/security attributes and enforce address ranges so debug, DMA-like peripherals, or external-facing controllers cannot access protected memory/MMIO outside their authorized regions.
